---
title: "passess"
subtitle: "Analytical solutions to the Poisson equation for assessing numerical solvers"
---

`passess` provides closed-form solutions to the gravitational Poisson
problem

$$ \nabla^2 \psi = -4\pi\,\gamma\,\rho $$

for a handful of geometries that are convenient for benchmarking numerical
solvers: 2D Cartesian, 2D polar, 3D Cartesian, and 3D spherical. The
package is deliberately narrow in scope; its job is to hand you a
reference $\psi$ that a numerical solver can be compared against.

## Where to start

Two worked examples walk through the typical workflow of picking a
geometry, instantiating a solver, and plotting the resulting density
and potential side by side.

- [2D polar — mass anomaly in a cylindrical shell](examples/dim2-polar.ipynb)
- [2D Cartesian — mass anomaly in a depth layer](examples/dim2-cartesian.ipynb)

Full derivations live next to the code on GitHub:

- [solution-dim2-polar.md](https://github.com/sghelichkhani/passess/blob/main/solution-dim2-polar.md)
- [solution-dim2-cartesian.md](https://github.com/sghelichkhani/passess/blob/main/solution-dim2-cartesian.md)
- [solution-dim3-spherical.md](https://github.com/sghelichkhani/passess/blob/main/solution-dim3-spherical.md)
- [solution-dim3-cartesian.md](https://github.com/sghelichkhani/passess/blob/main/solution-dim3-cartesian.md)

The [API reference](reference/index.qmd) documents every public class in
the package.

## Installation

```bash
pip install git+https://github.com/sghelichkhani/passess.git
```

To build the documentation locally, install the `docs` extras and then
render with Quarto:

```bash
pip install -e '.[docs]'
cd docs
quartodoc build
quarto preview
```
